Cornell University College of Engineering Deans Office records, [ca. 1916-2000]
Collection Number: 16-2-2156

Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Cornell University College of Engineering Deans Office records, [ca. 1916-2000]
Collection Number: 16-2-2156

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Cornell University College of Engineering Deans Office records, [ca. 1916-2000]
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
16-2-2156
Abstract:
Dean's Office subject files, including correspondence, annual reports, minutes of meetings, and working papers concerning the establishment of programs and construction of buildings. Includes the administrative papers of Deans Andrew Schultz, William Streett, T. E. Everhart, Edmund Cranch, and Solomon Hollister. Earlier records consist primarily of the records of Assistant Engineering Dean John F. McManus. Also, blank Fuertes Medal for graduate research in civil engineering.
Creator:
Cornell University. College of Engineering
Quanitities:
105.5 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Dean's Office subject files, including correspondence, annual reports, minutes of meetings, and working papers concerning the establishment of programs and construction of buildings. Includes the administrative papers of Deans Andrew Schultz, William Streett, T. E. Everhart, Edmund Cranch, and Solomon Hollister. Earlier records consist primarily of the records of Assistant Engineering Dean John F. McManus. Also, blank Fuertes Medal for graduate research in civil engineering. Includes the Thurston Hall Cornerstone Time Capsule.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Cornell University College of Engineering Dean's Office records, #16-2-2156. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:

Access restricted to the permission of the office of origin, except for Box 106, folders 1-85, and Box 115.
Conditions Governing Use

Box 115 contains sharp metal. It underwent mold remediation but retains a strong musty odor.
SUBJECTS

Names:
McManus, John F.
Schultz, Andrew Schultz, 1913-
Streett, William B. (William Bernard), 1932-
Everhart, Thomas E.
Cranch, Edmund T. (Edmund Titus), 1922-
Hollister, Solomon Cady, 1891-
Subjects:
Engineering -- Study and teaching.

Box 115
Thurston Hall time capsule
 1939-1952
Scope and Contents
Note: box contains sharp metal and a strong mold odor. Removed from Thurston Hall during construction in 2023. Contents include: various manuals and copies of engineering books; program of the cornerstone ceremony; list of the Board of Trustee members; student and staff directories; a copy of the 1952 Widow, The Sun, The Ithaca Journal, and The New York Times; and other Cornell programs.
